steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence contains an opening em-dash before "the International Criminal Police ______". That phrase is an appositive renaming Interpol. An appositive set off by an opening dash must be closed by a matching dash before the sentence continues with "in 1961".

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Organization—': closes the appositive with a matching em-dash, correctly mirroring the opening dash. The parenthetical is complete and the sentence reads cleanly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Organization:': a colon does not match an opening dash. Using mismatched punctuation marks breaks the parenthetical structure. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Organization,': a comma is too weak to close what an em-dash opened, and creates inconsistent punctuation around the appositive. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Organization': no closing punctuation leaves the appositive unclosed, making it impossible to tell where the parenthetical ends and the main clause resumes. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
